response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion:
As an 88-year-old retired man living in Philadelphia, I believe that the best way to manage and deal with fake content on social media is to approach it with a critical and discerning mindset. Here are some tips that have helped me navigate this issue: 
1. Be aware of confirmation bias: When consuming information on social media, be aware of your own biases and try to seek out diverse perspectives. This will help you avoid the trap of only listening to views that confirms what you already believe. 
2. Verify sources: Before sharing or believing any information on social media, make sure to verify its source. Check if the information is coming from a reputable news organization or if it's just a random post. 
3. Use fact-checking websites: There are several fact-checking websites that can help you verify the accuracy of information on social media. Some popular ones include Snopes, FactCheck.org, and PolitiFact. 
4. Be cautious of sensational headlines: Many fake news articles have attention-grabbing headlines that are designed to get your attention. Be cautious of these headlines and take the time to read the article before sharing or believing it. 
5. Use social media platforms' built-in tools: Most social media platforms have built-in tools that can help you manage fake content. For example, Facebook has a feature that allows you to flag suspicious posts, and Twitter has a feature that allows you to report spam accounts. 
6. Be cautious of memes: Memes are often used to spread misinformation, so be cautious of them on social media. Before sharing a meme, make sure to verify its accuracy and sources. 
7. Take breaks from social media: Finally, take breaks from social media to avoid feeling overwhelmed or burnt out. Taking breaks can help you maintain a healthy perspective and avoid getting caught up in fake news and misinformation. 
In conclusion, managing and dealing with fake content on social media requires a critical and discerning mindset. By following these tips, you can navigate the complex landscape of social media and stay informed without falling prey to misinformation.
